name: Dutch B1 Vocabulary
description: Intermediate Dutch — CEFR B1 level vocabulary

facts.explanation: aandacht — attention. Also: respect, affection, meditation (contemplative discourse)
absoluut — absolute, complete, perfect. Also: absolute, absolutist, absolutistic (pertaining to the absolute sovereignty of rulers)
actie — action, activity, movement; especially (without article) a fast-paced activity. Also: action; motion, legal charge; formal claim, share in an enterprise
activiteit — activity (state of being active). Also: activity (something with which one is occupied), activity (movement)
ambtenaar — civil servant.
anderstalig — relating to another language. Also: speaking another language
arts — physician, doctor.
bar — harsh, tough (used mainly with koude (“cold”), or omstandigheden (“conditions”)). Also: barren, inhospitable, bare, crude, grim, unfriendly
begin — start, beginning.
behoren — to belong (be accepted in a group). Also: to befit, behoove
belang — interest, concern. Also: importance, significance
belasting — strain, burden, load. Also: tax, taxation, load
beperken — to limit, to curtail, to restrict, to constrain. Also: to abridge
beslissen — to decide, to determine. Also: to decide, to make a definitive choice (between options)
besteden — to spend. Also: to match for a job
bestuur — management. Also: governing board, reign
beuk — a beech, tree of the genus Fagus. Also: common beech (Fagus sylvatica)
bevolking — population (the actual group of residents of an area; not the number/statistic thereof).
bewaren — to preserve, keep. Also: to guard, watch over
bibliotheek — library.
blijken — to appear, to become apparent. Also: to appear, to turn out
blik — a glance. Also: a manner of looking, a ray, a beam
bouwen — to build, to construct. Also: to cultivate, to till
bovendien — moreover, furthermore. Also: besides, in addition
buffel — a buffalo, one of some mammals of the subfamily Bovinae, especially of the genus Bubalus or species Syncerus caffer.
buitenland — all foreign countries, collectively; anything abroad.
buitenlander — foreigner, alien (person from outside one's country). Also: any person of non-European descent; see also allochtoon for further characterization
buitenlands — foreign, exterior.

bureau — desk. Also: office, office building (especially office buildings for writing and publishing journalism and literature, or the police station as a building), agency (government organisation, such as a police station: "politiebureau"; a scientific or humanities research institute: "onderzoeksbureau"; political party think tank: "wetenschappelijk bureau"), company (in design, engineering, journalism, communications, marketing, market/opinion research), agency (doing business for another, such as an advertising agency: "reclamebureau"; a news agency: "persbureau")
burger — citizen, civilian. Also: middle-class or bourgeois person, burgher
buur — neighbour.
campagne — campaign.
cd — CD, compact disc.
centrum — the centre, middle of focal part (e.g. of activity). Also: centre (UK), a centre, centralised facility; also, gathering place, city centre, town centre
cijfer — digit, numeral. Also: statistic, figure, a grade given for an assignment or class, particularly in primary school
cultureel — cultural.

cultuur — culture (practices and beliefs particular to a society or group). Also: culture (arts, historic heritage and creative media as a sector), culture (cultivation, tillage of crops), culture (collective of microorganisms, such as artificially bred bacteria or fungi)
dagelijks — daily.
dak — roof.
delen — to divide, to split up. Also: to share, to divide
dienst — service. Also: draft
diligence — a diligence, a stage-coach.
discussie — discussion, debate.
docent — teacher, docent.
drug — a recreational drug, psychoactive substance, especially when illegal and addictive.
drukken — to press. Also: to print, to hide (by pressing its body close to the ground), to shirk, to defecate
echter — however.
economisch — economic, relating to the economy. Also: economic, relating to economics, economical, relating to economizing
eenvoudig — simple, plain. Also: simple, easy, simple, simplex (not composite)
eenzaam — lonely, lonesome.
energie — energy.
enkel — single. Also: only, sole
enthousiast — one who is inspired or possessed by a deity; esp. one inspired by God. Also: a fanatic
euthanasie — euthanasia (medically assisted killing). Also: any gentle or good death
excuus — an excuse, apology, explanation to avoid or alleviate guilt or negative judgement. Also: an excuse, pretext
fietsen — to ride or cycle a bicycle, to bike.
fietser — cyclist.
file — traffic jam. Also: queue
financieel — financial.
foto — photo, photograph.
fout — wrong. Also: unfashionable, cheesy, inappropriate, active in or collaborating with far-right movements, especially Nazism
gebied — area, region. Also: domain, subject area, order, commandment
gebouw — building, structure, construction.
gebruik — use. Also: custom, practice
gedicht — poem.
gedrag — behaviour, conduct.
gelden — to apply, to be applicable, to be in effect. Also: to count (be estimated as valid, useful, or influential), to be considered [with als ‘as’], to be considered to be
gemeente — municipality, commune. Also: congregation
geschikt — adequate, suitable, appropriate. Also: friendly, amiable (when talking about a person)
gevolg — consequence, result. Also: entourage
geweld — violence. Also: strong or violent force; ferocity
glas — glass (material). Also: glass (vessel), glass (quantity)
gouverneur — governor (e.g. of a province).
grappig — funny.
gratis — free, without charge.
grens — physical, territorial border, frontier. Also: boundary, limit, threshold
groeien — to grow physically. Also: to increase
handelaar — merchant, trader.
handig — handy, convenient, practical. Also: handy, capable, dexterous, clever, crafty, cunning, resourceful, clever
helft — half.
hoeveelheid — quantity, amount.
houding — attitude. Also: stance, posture, pose
huisdier — domestic animal. Also: pet
huishouden — to do housekeeping; keep house. Also: to ravage, make a shambles
humor — humour (sense of amusement). Also: humour (bodily fluid)
inlichting — intelligence, source of information.
intelligentie — intelligence (capacity of mind).
interessant — interesting, arousing or holding the attention of; fascinating. Also: notable, important, profitable, beneficial
interesse — interest (e.g. in a subject).
interesseren — to interest (to attract attention or concern). Also: to show interest
inwoner — inhabitant.
jaarlijks — yearly, annual.
kaas — cheese.
kennis — knowledge.
kilometer — kilometre.
klacht — complaint (general, also physical).
koelkast — a refrigerator, a fridge. Also: an unfashionable, large, bulky cell phone
koffer — a suitcase. Also: a luggage compartment in a small vehicle; a trunk, a boot, a trunk, a large chest used to move luggage
kost — cost, expenses. Also: board, livelihood, meals and lodgings, food, nourishment
kunstenaar — artist.
kussen — to kiss, to give a kiss to. Also: to practice kissing
kwaliteit — quality.
kwart — a quarter, one of four (equal) parts.
leiden — to lead, to take the lead. Also: to guide, to lead, to go, to follow a path to
lelijk — ugly.
leveren — to supply. Also: to deliver
lezer — a reader, person who reads a text. Also: a reader, device to read one or more parameters, often numerically expressed, or a whole encrypted (e.g. audio-visual) file
lid — member (of a group). Also: member, limb (extremity of a body), member, paragraph, subsection (legislative drafting), article, particularly in the Southern diminutive form ledeken
lijst — list.
maatschappelijk — societal. Also: social
maatschappij — company. Also: society
mama — mama, mother.
medelijden — compassion, sympathy for the suffering of another.
melden — to report, to communicate; let know. Also: to inform, to let know, to give an update
milieu — environment. Also: milieu
minstens — at least.
moeite — effort. Also: difficulty
mogelijkheid — possibility.
nat — wet.
noorden — north.

nummer — number used as a means of identification. Also: number (used to show the rank of something in a list or sequence), a song or musical performance; a track, issue of a publication (e.g. a magazine), number of an item on a menu; (by extension) a dish identified by such a number
oefenen — to practice, train.
officieel — official.
onderwerp — subject, topic. Also: subject (of a sentence)
ongeval — accident.
ontstaan — to arise, to come into being. Also: to elude, remain out of reach
ontvangen — to receive.
oorlog — war.
oorzaak — cause.
openbaar — public. Also: non-affiliated (e.g. neither Catholic, Protestant, nor socialist)

opnemen — to pick up, to raise. Also: to pick up or answer (the phone or horn), to take in, to admit, to reserve a spot for, to record, to snap (a photo), shoot (a film), to eyeball, to look at, to observe or measure (result), to begin, to initiate
opnieuw — again.
organisatie — an organization (institution). Also: organisation, the way or degree that something is organised
organiseren — to organise (UK); organize (US).
overheid — the government, the authorities (the state and its administration; governmental administration in the widest sense).
overlijden — to die.
parkeerplaats — a parking space (space for one vehicle). Also: a parking lot, a car park (several spaces for vehicles)
parlement — parliament. Also: conversation, talking, discussion, talk, meeting, assembly
partij — party, quantity. Also: party, group of people, side or actor in a conflict, procedure et cetera, political party, party, festivity
periode — period (of time). Also: era, period (length of a repeating interval of a periodic function or repeating decimal)
personeel — staff (employees).
persoonlijk — personal.

pil — a pill, a usually ball- or oval-shaped, coated portion of a drug to be taken orally. Also: 'the' contraceptive pill, an analogous ball-shaped object, an expert in pill use:, a pharmacist, an MD, a med student, a thick sandwich, a hard kick on a balk or other object, a bitter experience, a large book, a tome
politiek — politics.
populair — popular.
produceren — to produce (bring forth). Also: to produce (music or film)
programma — program.
provincie — province. Also: countryside, hinterland
radio — radio, tuner.
reageren — to react, to respond. Also: to react, to undergo a chemical reaction
rechter — judge.
reclame — publicity. Also: advertisement or ads, the stock that is advertised at a discount
reden — to equip (a ship). Also: to equip, fit, supply with (something with a supplemental tool)
regel — line of writing, verse; (pre-printed) straight line to write it on. Also: rule, regulation, custom, prescript; the statute of an order etc., narrow slat; especially a ruler
regenen — to rain, to precipitate. Also: to accumulate in large amounts, as though by rain, to pour down, to fall out of the sky in large amounts
regering — governing (act of; ruling a state or similar political entity). Also: government (a governing council: especially the executive branch of government), rule, reign (of a prince etc.)

rekening — a calculation. Also: a mathematically consistent set of data in accounting, a client's account at a bank etc., Reckoning, account, justification, a bill, a check; hence the cost of something, an invoice
relatie — relation. Also: relationship
relatief — relative.
resultaat — result.
richting — direction. Also: movement, tendency
risico — risk.
roken — to smoke (a tobacco product, another drug, a surrogate, a foodstuff). Also: to smoke, to produce smoke
rol — role, function (as in actions or tasks that a person or a group is expected to perform). Also: role, character
ruimte — space, room (for a physical object to occupy). Also: room (in a building), space, outer space
samenleving — society (long-standing group of people sharing cultural aspects; people of one’s country or community taken as a whole). Also: cohabitation
samenvatting — a summary, a digest.
scheiden — to separate. Also: to divorce
schrijver — writer, author. Also: scribe, clerk
sigaret — cigarette.
single — a single (short music record, e.g. 45 RPM vinyl with an A side and a B side; main track of such a record). Also: a single (person without a romantic partner)
situatie — situation, circumstance.
slachtoffer — victim. Also: sacrificial victim, usually an animal
slechts — only, just.
snikken — to sob.
sociaal — social.
sport — a sport; (uncountable) sports.
staat — state (political entity). Also: state, condition, status, table, list, chart, plantation
staren — to gaze, stare.
stevig — firm, sturdy. Also: sizeable, considerable
stijgen — to climb, to go up, to rise. Also: to ascend
stimuleren — to stimulate. Also: to encourage, to promote
stralen — to radiate, to shine. Also: to miscarry, to hit with an arrow or similar weapon, to prick, to sting, to squirt out
streek — region. Also: stroke (movement e.g. with a paintbrush), prank, trick, antic
streep — stripe. Also: a short stroke (e.g. of the pen), hyphen
streng — strict, severe, rigorous, unbending, cruel.

studie — the activity of studying (mental effort to learn or acquire knowledge). Also: a study (tertiary education, usually at an academic level), a study, (piece of) research, a study, preliminary sketch, model ... in preparation for a work of art or as an exercise in a technique
succes — success.
tegenwoordig — present (in time), current. Also: physically present
tekst — text. Also: lyrics, a large size of type standardized as 16 point
tellen — to count, to enumerate. Also: to determine the number of, to matter, to be of significance
thema — theme, topic, subject, issue. Also: theme
tiener — teenager.
titel — title.

toneel — stage, scene, place for a performance in front of an assembled audience. Also: scene, depiction on stage (often in the diminutive) or part of a performance, theatre, the dramatical arts, theatre, site or area where something (a crime, battle...) happens
tonen — to show, to demonstrate.
trouwens — besides; by the way.
uiteindelijk — eventual. Also: final
uitleg — explanation. Also: expansion, esp. the expansion of a city outside its previous walls
uitnodigen — to invite.
verbeteren — to improve. Also: to correct
verdriet — grief, sadness, sorrow. Also: misery, distress, chagrin, aversion
vereniging — association. Also: unification, union
verkeer — traffic. Also: intercourse, exchange, dealings
verkeerd — wrong. Also: distorted
verlangen — to long for, desire, yearn. Also: to expect, to ask (of someone)
veroorzaken — to cause.
verschijnen — to appear, show up. Also: to be published
verschil — difference.
vervelen — to annoy, to pester. Also: to bore; inspire boredom in somebody, to be bored
vervoer — the act or process of transporting; transportation. Also: any means of transport, vehicle and/or driver etc.
verzamelen — to collect, to gather.
verzekering — insurance. Also: life insurance, assurance, act of assuring, something that one has assured
voeren — to guide, lead. Also: to wage (war), to bear a coat of arms, to bear a name unofficially or semi-officially (e.g. in the Middle Ages when names were not entirely fixed yet).
volwassen — adult, full-grown.
voorkomen — to exist, to occur. Also: to happen, to appear, to get ahead; to draw ahead
voorstellen — to present, to put forth, to introduce. Also: to propose, to suggest, to imagine, to amount to, to matter, to be important, to represent, to depict
voorstelling — show, performance. Also: presentation, notion, idea; mental picture
vormen — to form, mold. Also: to make up, constitute, to originate, to educate
vriendschap — friendship.
waarschijnlijk — probably.
website — web site.
weigeren — to refuse, to deny (not to allow).
wensen — to wish.
werkgever — an employer.
wet — law (rule). Also: law (body of rules declared and/or enforced by a government), law of nature
woning — house, abode, residence, dwelling.
zeer — a physical pain, ache, hurt. Also: grief, suffering, a sore spot; crust on a wound, boil etc., a cause of physical pain, notably disease; discomfort, uneasiness, a flaw, fault, sin, defect
zilveren — silvern, silver; made of silver.
zuiden — south. Also: southern, from the south
zwak — weak. Also: shabby
zwembad — swimming pool.

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
